role: MEK member; killed by Peykar Organisation
bio: Majid Sharif-Vaghefi was a member of the Mojahedin-e Khalq (MEK) who was murdered in May 1975 by the Marxist faction led by [Taghi Shahram](/people/taghi-shahram). His killing was the most infamous act in the violent internal purge that accompanied the ideological shift of the MEK's Marxist wing — a purge that transformed the MEK's Marxist faction into the [Peykar Organization](/orgs/peykar-organization). Sharif-Vaghefi was targeted because he remained committed to the MEK's Islamic ideology and refused to accept the Marxist faction's ideological conversion. His murder alongside fellow MEK member Morteza Samadiyeh-Labbaf became a symbol of the brutal internecine violence within the Iranian left of the 1970s and is central to understanding the MEK/Peykar split.
editorial_notes: Killed May 1975 by Taghi Shahram's Marxist faction within MEK. Death central to MEK/Peykar split history.
